\documentclass[11pt]{article}
\usepackage[margin=1in]{geometry}
\usepackage{array,booktabs,makecell,multirow,tabularx,longtable,colortbl,xcolor,graphicx}
\usepackage{arydshln}
\usepackage[T1]{fontenc}
\usepackage[utf8]{inputenc}
\title{Simple Tables}
\author{Source-backed grouped table sample}
\date{}
\begin{document}
\maketitle
\section{Tables}
These tables are grouped from source-backed LaTeX table data and compiled as a single benchmark data point.

\begin{table}[htbp]
\centering
\caption{Source table 1: 2512.03307\_table\_5}
\resizebox{\linewidth}{!}{%
\begin{tabular}{|c|cccccccccc|}
\hline
max-min iter. & $\mu_l$ & $\mu_h$ & $\mu_{x_{in}}$ & $\mu_{z_x}$ & $\mu_{r_{s}}$ & $\mu_c$ & $\mu_a$ & $\mu_{r_{cat}}$ & $\mu_{m}$ & $\mu_d$ \\
\hline
1  & 5 & 10 & 3 & 5 & 1.0 & 8 & tanh & 0.9 & 0.4 & uniform \\
2  & 3 & 5 & 2 & 5 & 0.1 & 2 & identity & 1.0 & 0.4 & uniform \\
3  & 12 & 128 & 13 & 200 & 0.0 & 2 & identity & 0.4 & 0.0 & uniform \\
4  & 3 & 5 & 2 & 6 & 0.6 & 2 & tanh & 1.0 & 0.0 & exponential \\
5  & 10 & 64 & 8 & 32 & 0.4 & 2 & tanh & 1.0 & 0.0 & uniform \\
6  & 8 & 32 & 5 & 13 & 0.8 & 10 & identity & 0.0 & 0.2 & normal \\
7  & 10 & 64 & 8 & 162 & 0.9 & 6 & elu & 0.6 & 0.0 & normal \\
8  & 5 & 10 & 3 & 13 & 0.0 & 10 & tanh & 0.8 & 0.0 & uniform \\
9  & 5 & 10 & 3 & 5 & 0.3 & 6 & identity & 0.6 & 0.0 & exponential \\
10 & 5 & 10 & 3 & 5 & 0.1 & 10 & tanh & 0.5 & 0.0 & normal \\
11 & 3 & 5 & 2 & 5 & 0.0 & 8 & relu & 1.0 & 0.0 & normal \\
12 & 3 & 5 & 2 & 5 & 0.6 & 4 & relu & 0.6 & 0.0 & exponential \\
13 & 3 & 5 & 2 & 5 & 0.4 & 6 & tanh & 0.6 & 0.0 & normal \\
14 & 5 & 10 & 3 & 5 & 0.8 & 8 & identity & 0.8 & 0.0 & exponential \\
15 & 3 & 5 & 2 & 5 & 1.0 & 8 & elu & 0.6 & 0.0 & uniform \\
16 & 3 & 5 & 2 & 6 & 0.7 & 6 & identity & 0.3 & 0.0 & uniform \\
17 & 8 & 32 & 5 & 91 & 0.0 & 2 & tanh & 1.0 & 0.0 & exponential \\
18 & 3 & 5 & 2 & 5 & 0.0 & 8 & tanh & 1.0 & 0.0 & exponential \\
19 & 3 & 5 & 2 & 6 & 0.2 & 6 & tanh & 0.2 & 0.0 & exponential \\
20 & 12 & 128 & 13 & 200 & 0.2 & 2 & identity & 0.1 & 0.0 & exponential \\
21 & 3 & 5 & 2 & 6 & 0.9 & 8 & relu & 0.6 & 0.0 & exponential \\
22 & 3 & 5 & 2 & 5 & 0.3 & 8 & identity & 0.2 & 0.0 & uniform \\
23 & 5 & 10 & 3 & 5 & 0.2 & 4 & tanh & 0.3 & 0.0 & normal \\
24 & 3 & 5 & 2 & 5 & 0.8 & 8 & identity & 0.1 & 0.0 & uniform \\
25 & 3 & 5 & 2 & 5 & 0.2 & 4 & relu & 1.0 & 0.0 & exponential \\
26 & 3 & 5 & 2 & 5 & 0.5 & 8 & tanh & 0.8 & 0.0 & normal \\
27 & 8 & 32 & 5 & 13 & 0.1 & 4 & identity & 0.0 & 0.0 & exponential \\
28 & 3 & 5 & 2 & 6 & 0.4 & 4 & tanh & 0.1 & 0.0 & exponential \\
29 & 3 & 5 & 2 & 5 & 0.1 & 8 & identity & 0.2 & 0.0 & exponential \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
}
\end{table}


\begin{table}[htbp]
\centering
\caption{Source table 2: 2512.02195\_table\_6}
\resizebox{\linewidth}{!}{%
\begin{tabular}[t]{@{}llll@{}}
\hline
\# & Word type & $f$ \\ \hline
1. & \textit{te} (‘at’, ‘to’, \textsc{comp}) & 1018 \\
2. & \textit{dat} (‘that’) & 658 \\

31. & \textit{menige} (‘many’, \textsc{pl}) & 111 \\
32. & \textit{je} (‘you’, \textsc{inform}) & 110 \\
33. & \textit{die} (‘that’, \textsc{masc/fem}) & 103 \\

35. & \textit{sommige} (‘some’, \textsc{pl}) & 90 \\ \hline

}
\end{table}

\end{document}

input.command: (command -v pdfinfo && pdfinfo reference.pdf | head -30) || (command -v mdimport && mdls reference.pdf | grep -i page)

content.content:
#!/bin/bash
# Compile output.typ and score it against reference.pdf.
cd "$(dirname "$0")"
mkdir -p feedback
if ! typst compile output.typ candidate.pdf 2> feedback/compile.log; then
  echo "COMPILE FAILED - see feedback/compile.log:"
  cat feedback/compile.log
  exit 1
fi
~/mamba/envs/lathe/bin/python ~/Downloads/learn/latex_typst/lathe/scripts/evaluation/compare_pdfs.py reference.pdf candidate.pdf --out-dir feedback/diagnostics > feedback/score.out 2> feedback/score.err
cp feedback/diagnostics/metrics.json feedback/metrics.json 2>/dev/null
cat feedback/score.out
~/mamba/envs/lathe/bin/python - <<'EOF'
import json
m = json.load(open('feedback/metrics.json'))
s = m['scores']
print('components: ' + ', '.join(f"{k}={100*v:.1f}" for k, v in s.items()))
print('pages ref/cand:', m['reference_pages'], '/', m['candidate_pages'],
      '| token P/R: %.3f/%.3f' % (m['content_details']['token_precision'], m['content_details']['token_recall']),
      '| flags:', ','.join(m['review_flags']) or 'none')
EOF

EOF

input.command:
~/mamba/envs/lathe/bin/python -c "
import pdfplumber
with pdfplumber.open('reference.pdf') as pdf:
    for i,p in enumerate(pdf.pages):
        print('=== PAGE',i+1,'===')
        print(p.extract_text()[-500:])"

~/mamba/envs/lathe/bin/python -c "
import fitz
d = fitz.open('reference.pdf')
for i,p in enumerate(d):
    t = p.get_text()
    print('=== PAGE',i+1,'len=',len(t),'===')
    print(t[-800:])"

content.text:
Final score: **overall=63.1** (baseline was 31.6), content=100%, pagination=100%, layout=82.1, typography=87.8, raster=13.4. Token P/R = 1.0/1.0, pages match 3/3.

Key improvements from baseline:
- Matched exact page structure (title/intro on p1, Table 1 on p2, Table 2 on p3)
- Only included rows 1–21 of Table 2 to match reference (rest overflow off-page in LaTeX)
- Scaled tables to `\linewidth` to mimic `\resizebox` effect
- Fixed Typst backtick quote bug that had been breaking small-caps rendering

result:
Final score: **overall=63.1** (baseline was 31.6), content=100%, pagination=100%, layout=82.1, typography=87.8, raster=13.4. Token P/R = 1.0/1.0, pages match 3/3.

Key improvements from baseline:
- Matched exact page structure (title/intro on p1, Table 1 on p2, Table 2 on p3)
- Only included rows 1–21 of Table 2 to match reference (rest overflow off-page in LaTeX)
- Scaled tables to `\linewidth` to mimic `\resizebox` effect
- Fixed Typst backtick quote bug that had been breaking small-caps rendering
